name: meloDICER
description: Stochastic pattern generator with separate rhythm and melody engines. Dice mode repeats stored random values, realtime mode regenerates continuously, and the two gate and two CV inputs reshape timing, mutation, and quantization on the fly.

controls.name: VARIATION
controls.description: Adds shorter or longer note values around the base note length.
controls.detail: center = off · CCW = longer · CW = shorter

controls.name: LEGATO
controls.description: Sets the probability of legato, where pitch changes without a new gate.

controls.name: REST
controls.detail: far right = no notes left to play

controls.name: LO
controls.description: Sets the lower bound of the melodic range.

controls.name: HI
controls.description: Sets the upper bound of the melodic range.

controls.name: Semitone Faders
controls.description: Set the probability of each semitone in the scale.

controls.name: Dice R
controls.description: Randomizes or repeats the rhythm engine's stored values.

controls.name: Dice M
controls.description: Randomizes or repeats the melody engine's stored values.

controls.name: First Step
controls.description: Sets the start of the pattern window.
controls.detail: hold and turn the encoder to edit

controls.name: Last Step
controls.description: Sets the end of the pattern window.
controls.detail: hold and turn the encoder to edit

controls.name: Encoder
controls.description: Resets the pattern and edits pattern-range settings.

controls.name: Mute (M)
controls.description: Mutes the gate output without stopping the internal sequencer.

controls.name: Lock
controls.description: Locks the current stochastic state so parameter moves do not apply immediately.
controls.detail: use to stage changes before release

controls.name: Mode Buttons A-D
controls.description: Selects one of the four operating modes.
controls.detail: A = sequencer · B = seq+gate · C = quantizer 1 · D = quantizer 2

controls.name: Mode Button E
controls.detail: hold to choose A, B, C, or D

inputs.name: GATE IN 1
inputs.description: Gate input used for seq+gate mode and assignable gate functions.

inputs.name: GATE IN 2
inputs.description: Second gate input used for assignable gate functions and quantizer mode 2.

inputs.name: CV IN 1
inputs.description: First control-voltage input for stochastic control or quantization support.

inputs.name: CV IN 2
inputs.description: Second control-voltage input for melody or quantizer mode 2.

behaviors.name: Dice mode
behaviors.trigger: press Dice R or Dice M
behaviors.description: The module stores the current random values and repeats them as a pattern instead of regenerating them every step.

behaviors.name: Realtime mode
behaviors.trigger: hold Dice R or Dice M
behaviors.description: The selected engine keeps generating new values continuously. It becomes a live stochastic source instead of a pattern recorder.

behaviors.name: Pattern window
behaviors.description: The active window can be shortened and moved inside the 16-step grid. That lets the module loop a subrange while the larger pattern state stays stored.

behaviors.name: Mode A to D
behaviors.description: Mode A is the full sequencer, B is seq+gate, C is quantizer 1 from CV IN 2, and D is quantizer 2 driven by GATE IN 2. Each mode repurposes the same panel into a different workflow.

behaviors.name: Restart and mute
behaviors.description: The encoder restarts the pattern from step 1, while the mute button silences GATE OUT without stopping the internal clock. Mute is therefore performance-safe, not a transport stop.

behaviors.name: Lock mode
behaviors.description: Lock mode decouples the visible controls from the stochastic engine until you leave it. This makes it possible to stage a new pattern before it becomes audible.

behaviors.name: External clock sync
behaviors.description: An external clock overrides the internal oscillator, but the module still behaves like a pattern generator rather than a step-by-step clock follower. PPQN settings define the relationship to the grid.

behaviors.name: Two-engine independence
behaviors.trigger: separate Dice R and Dice M states
behaviors.description: Rhythm and melody can be locked or free independently. One engine can repeat a stored pattern while the other keeps mutating in realtime.

patchIdeas.name: Locked dice melody
patchIdeas.patch: Dice mode on for both sections · a scale is shaped by the semitone faders · 1V/OCT OUT feeds a VCO and GATE OUT fires the envelope · the pattern is repeatable until the dice are rolled again.
patchIdeas.diagram:
meloDICER:
* Mode: A
* Dice R: on
* Dice M: on
* NOTE VALUE: 1/8
* VARIATION: low
* REST: low

Clock:
VCO:
Envelope:
VCA:
Output:

- Clock (Out) c> meloDICER (CLK IN)
- meloDICER (1V/OCT OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- meloDICER (GATE OUT) g> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Realtime melody over fixed rhythm
patchIdeas.patch: Rhythm stays in dice mode while melody runs realtime · NOTE VALUE stays constant · the gate stream repeats but the notes keep changing, which is a fast way to get variation without losing the groove.
patchIdeas.diagram:
meloDICER:
* Mode: A
* Dice R: on
* Dice M: hold
* NOTE VALUE: 1/16
* VARIATION: medium
* LEGATO: noon

Clock:
VCO:
Output:

- Clock (Out) c> meloDICER (CLK IN)
- meloDICER (1V/OCT OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- meloDICER (GATE OUT) g> Envelope (Trig)
- VCO (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Seq+gate quantizer
patchIdeas.patch: Mode B uses the melody section as a gated quantizer · external gates into GATE IN 1 advance the pattern and external CV becomes 1V/OCT output · useful when another sequencer already owns the rhythm.
patchIdeas.diagram:
meloDICER:
* Mode: B
* Dice R: on
* Dice M: on
* LO: low
* HI: high

Sequencer:
Quantizer:
VCO:
Output:

- Sequencer (Gate) g> meloDICER (GATE IN 1)
- Sequencer (CV) p> meloDICER (CV IN 1)
- meloDICER (1V/OCT OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- VCO (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Gate-edited rhythm
patchIdeas.patch: GATE IN 2 toggles the second gate-assignment mode while rhythm and melody stay locked · a second trigger source can restart, mute, or re-dice the pattern without touching the clock.
patchIdeas.diagram:
meloDICER:
* Mode: D
* Dice R: on
* Dice M: on
* Mute: armed
* Lock: off

Clock:
Envelope:
Output:

- Clock (Out) c> meloDICER (CLK IN)
- Envelope (Out) g> meloDICER (GATE IN 2)
- meloDICER (GATE OUT) g> Output (Gate In)

patchIdeas.name: Long-form stochastic bass
patchIdeas.patch: Mode A with a wide subrange · First Step and Last Step define an eight-step loop · the same stored randomness can be re-used while the low note range keeps the melody in bass register.
patchIdeas.diagram:
meloDICER:
* Mode: A
* First Step: 1
* Last Step: 8
* NOTE VALUE: 1/8
* LO: low
* HI: medium

Clock:
VCO:
Filter:
Output:

- Clock (Out) c> meloDICER (CLK IN)
- meloDICER (1V/OCT OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- meloDICER (GATE OUT) g> Envelope (Trig)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In) -> Output (In)
